name: Van de Graaf canon
description: Renaissance geometric page construction discovered by Jan Tschichold and rediscovered by Hendrik van de Graaf, dividing page diagonals into 9 segments to derive type area with 1:1.5:2 margin ratios. Universal across page sizes.
construction_method: Draw both page diagonals; divide page width by 9; intersect with diagonals to define text block. Type area sits asymmetrically with smaller inner margin and largest bottom margin, optimizing for codex spread reading.

name: Tschichold golden canon
description: Tschichold's modernization of medieval canons in The Form of the Book (1975), preserving 2:3 page ratio with 9-division margins, derived from manuscripts of the high Middle Ages.
construction_method: Page proportion 2:3. Inner margin = 1 unit, top = 1, outer = 2, bottom = 3. Type block height equals page width. Text fits diagonals; ensures harmony with page proportions.

name: Rosarivo canon
description: Raul Rosarivo's 1947 empirical analysis of incunabula, establishing the geometric law that text height equals page width and proportional margin scheme 2:3:4:6.
construction_method: Divide page width by 9 to get base unit. Inner=2, top=3, outer=4, bottom=6 units. Text block proportions match the page itself, scaling fractally. Used in Gutenberg Bible analysis.

name: Villard de Honnecourt scheme
description: 13th century Gothic page construction by architect Villard de Honnecourt using diagonal subdivision to harmonize text with page proportions, predating Renaissance canons.
construction_method: Pages divided into 9 cells via diagonal lines forming X pattern. Text block occupies 6 of 9 cells in central position, leaving asymmetric margins favoring outer and lower areas for thumb space.

name: Le Corbusier Modulor
description: Le Corbusier's 1948 anthropomorphic measurement system applying golden ratio to human proportions for architecture and book design, creating modular scales of harmonious dimensions.
construction_method: Two scales (Red and Blue) based on average male body height (1.83m) and golden ratio. Dimensions cascade through phi multiplications. Applied to book grids, page heights, type sizes for total proportional harmony.

name: Gutenberg canon
description: 42-line Bible (1455) page construction following medieval canons of harmonic proportion, with text occupying ~50% of page area and asymmetric margins favoring outer.
construction_method: Two-column text block on 2:3 page. Inner margins minimized for binding, outer enlarged for thumb. Type area hugs upper-inner area, bottom margin largest. Established Western book design conventions.

name: Vitruvius proportions
description: Vitruvian classical architectural proportions from De Architectura applied to book pages, establishing geometric ratios derived from human body and golden mean.
construction_method: Apply golden ratio (phi=1.618) to page width:height. Text block follows phi or its inverse. Margins use Fibonacci sequence relationships. Roots all classical typography in classical order.

name: Greek classical golden ratio
description: Ancient Greek phi-based proportional system foundational to Western design, producing visually harmonious rectangles and divisions used in scrolls and later codex pages.
construction_method: Page or text block in 1:phi ratio. Cuts and subdivisions follow golden section. Margins reflect golden-ratio decreasing sequence. Foundation of Renaissance and modern proportional canons.

name: Roman classical canon
description: Imperial Roman scriptio continua page layout with capitalis quadrata script, no word spaces, generous margins, and stately uniform letter heights.
construction_method: Wide outer margins, narrow inner. Single column scriptio continua. Capital letters in uniform height. Wax tablets and papyrus scrolls dictated horizontal-only reading. Established imperial readability standards.

name: Carolingian minuscule canon
description: Charlemagne-era 8th-9th century reform unifying European script into clear minuscule with consistent x-height, word spacing, and balanced margins.
construction_method: Clear word separation introduced. Minuscule script with consistent baseline. Margins asymmetric with larger outer for marginalia. Established readable book hand basis for all Western typography.

name: Gothic textura canon
description: 13th-15th century Northern European Gothic black-letter page with dense vertical strokes, narrow columns, and minimal margins to economize parchment.
construction_method: Two narrow columns, tight leading. Textura quadrata with diamond serifs. Margins reduced versus Carolingian. High contrast between thick verticals and hairlines. Vellum economy favors density.

name: Renaissance Italianate
description: 15th-16th century Italian humanist book canon by Aldus Manutius and contemporaries, reviving Roman capital and minuscule with elegant margins and italic for portability.
construction_method: Single column. Roman or italic faces. Lower margin notably wider. Page size shrinks to enchiridion (pocket) format. Classical proportions in 1:phi or 2:3. Italic accommodates more text per line.

name: Jenson canon
description: Nicolas Jenson's 1470 Venetian Roman type page exemplifying classical proportions, used by Bruce Rogers as model for Centaur typeface and modern fine-press design.
construction_method: Single block, generous margins. Type area approximately 0.6× page. Bottom margin 1.5× top. Used phi divisions. Type x-height moderate, ascenders prominent. Foundation of fine-press tradition.

name: Aldus Manutius pocket book canon
description: Aldus's 1501 Octavo format introducing portable books with italic type and tight margins, democratizing reading and shaping book scales for centuries.
construction_method: Octavo format ~108×170mm. Italic typeface for line economy. Margins reduced for portability while preserving readability. Top binding allowed reading without table. Established travel-book conventions.

name: Bodoni 1818 canon
description: Giambattista Bodoni's Manuale Tipografico (1818) establishing Modern (Didone) type with extreme contrast, vertical stress, and stark white space typography.
construction_method: Wide margins emphasizing white space. Bodoni typeface with high stroke contrast. Single column, generous leading. Page proportions tend square. White space treated as integral design element.

name: Didot 1810 canon
description: Firmin Didot's French rationalist Modern type with vertical stress and high contrast, applied to neoclassical book design with measured geometric proportions.
construction_method: Symmetric layout with rationalized margins. Didot type with thin hairlines. Standardized page dimensions (Didot point 0.376mm). Classical balanced composition; foundation of French publishing.

name: Penguin 1935 standard canon
description: Edward Young's Penguin Books 1935 design with horizontal tri-band cover (color/title/black), Gill Sans typography, establishing mass-market paperback aesthetic.
construction_method: Horizontal three bands on cover (top color, white middle, bottom color). Gill Sans titling. Single-column body in Bembo or Pilgrim. Tight A-format paperback dimensions 110×178mm. Democratic, affordable design.

name: Penguin Composition Rules 1947
description: Jan Tschichold's 4-year tenure (1947-49) at Penguin establishing tight rules for type composition: spacing, ligatures, hyphenation, justification, capitalization.
construction_method: Tight letterspacing only on caps. Ligatures preserved. Em-dash with no space. Type size 11pt minimum. Generous leading. Single column with classical 2:3 page. Rules codified in 4-page memo, became typographic gospel.

name: Pelican Books canon
description: Penguin sister imprint (1937-1984) for serious nonfiction with distinctive light-blue covers, scholarly typography by Hans Schmoller (post-Tschichold), academic margins.
construction_method: Light-blue covers with white horizontal band. Times Roman or Pilgrim body type. Wider outer margins for note-taking. 11pt body, 13pt leading. Educational tone via spacious composition.

name: Faber & Faber editorial canon
description: Berthold Wolpe's 40-year (1941-75) Faber design tradition using Albertus titling with painterly covers, establishing British literary publisher aesthetic.
construction_method: Albertus titling for covers. Hand-lettered or typographic only covers (no images often). Body in Granjon, Bembo, Caslon. Generous margins for poetry collections. Square-ish format for verse.

name: 和漢古典版面（巻子本）
description: Heian-Muromachi era Japanese scroll page composition with vertical writing in distinct columns, kana flowing freely between fixed kanji rhythms, ink-shade variations.
construction_method: 縦書き複数列、行送りは流動的。漢字は固定リズム、仮名は連綿で流れる。墨の濃淡で強弱。料紙の継ぎ目を意識した構成。上下余白は装飾余白として機能し、左右は連続する。源氏物語絵巻等の伝統。

name: 写経古典版面（経典）
description: Buddhist sutra copying tradition with strict columnar composition, gold/silver mica grounds, vermilion punctuation, and precise vertical alignment for liturgical use.
construction_method: 縦17字×17列が標準（千字経等）。罫線は金泥または銀泥。写経独特の楷書体。朱の界線で行を区切る。上下余白は段ごとの呼吸を作る。料紙は紺紙金字、白紙黒字等。荘厳的な厳密性。

name: 江戸版本版面（黄表紙）
description: Edo period popular book genre (1775-1806) with woodblock printed integrated text-image compositions, kana-rich text flowing around illustrations.
construction_method: 半紙判、和綴じ。文字と絵が版面で一体化。仮名主体、絵を囲むように文が配置。物語の流れに応じて余白が変動。版木彫りの線質が活字より柔らかい。挿絵は文字と等価な情報。

name: 明治活版版面（東京活版）
description: Meiji-era introduction of Western movable type with first Japanese fonts (Tsukiji Type Foundry) establishing modern vertical typography conventions.
construction_method: 明朝体活字（東京築地活版製造所）。縦組み二段組が基本。約物処理を欧文に倣って規格化。上下マージンを欧米式に。漢字は明朝、仮名は変体仮名から平仮名へ統一。新聞・雑誌の規格を作った。

name: 写研写植版面（昭和）
description: Showa-era photo-typesetting (1960s-90s) by Shaken (Sha-Ken) enabling fine kerning, rubies, and vertical sophistication in magazines and books.
construction_method: 写研フォント（石井明朝、ナール、ゴナ等）。級数（Q）単位の精細な指定。手作業に近い字游間調整。雑誌の凝った組版が可能に。和文ルビの精緻な処理。1990s以降のDTP移行で衰退するが伝統技法は今も参照される。

name: 杉浦康平モジュラーグリッド
description: Sugiura Kohei's modular grid system synthesizing Swiss systematic design with East Asian cosmological ordering, applied to encyclopedic books and magazines.
construction_method: アジア的世界観をスイス・グリッドに統合。曼荼羅的多層構造。情報を等価レイヤーで配置。文字と図像を等価に扱うグリッド。『全宇宙誌』『遊』で実装。多重に重なるグリッドが情報の宇宙誌を構築する。

name: 鈴木一誌ページネーション基本
description: Suzuki Hitoshi's Pagination for the Basic Manual (1990s) systematizing 180 page design rules for Japanese editorial publishing, foundational reference.
construction_method: 180項目のページ設計ルール体系。版面率、字詰め、行送り、約物処理を体系化。メディアごとの最適値を提示。実務的かつ理論的。日本のエディトリアルデザインの理論的支柱。雑誌や書籍の現場マニュアルとして広く使われる。

name: Müller-Brockmann grid systems
description: Josef Müller-Brockmann's 1981 Grid Systems in Graphic Design codifying Swiss modular grids with mathematical precision, foundational for international typographic style.
construction_method: 列数・行数を数学的に決定。フィールド単位での情報配置。マージンとガターの厳密な比例。Akzidenz Grotesk/Helveticaを基本書体に。中立的・機能主義的。スイス派の聖典として現代まで参照される。

name: Marber Penguin Crime grid (1961)
description: Romek Marber's 1961 grid for Penguin Crime Series creating modular cover system with horizontal bands separating type, image, and price for instant brand recognition.
construction_method: カバー上3分の1にタイトル+著者ゴシック、中央に画像、下にロゴ・価格・シリーズ。Marber gridとして類書に展開。シリーズ性とブランド統一。Romek Marberが1961年に開発、シリーズ300+冊で実装。

name: Hofmann Basel canon
description: Armin Hofmann's Basel School pedagogical method emphasizing reduction, geometric simplicity, and rhythmic spacing in posters and book design (1947-1985).
construction_method: 幾何学的削減。ポイント・ライン・サーフェスの基本原理。リズミカルな空間配分。バーゼルスクールの教育課題と実践を融合。Akzidenz Grotesk中心。Wolfgang Weingartらに伝播し、ポスト・モダン理論へ展開。
